theme.title: Mixed / Stock-Picker's Market
theme.summary: Technology (+1.00%) and Financials (+0.87%) led the market today, while Consumer Staples (-1.01%) and Real Estate (-0.84%) brought up the rear. Growth sectors outpaced the rest, averaging +0.82% versus -0.84% for defensives and +0.16% for value, consistent with risk appetite favoring higher-beta names. Major indices moved in a tighter band — S&P 500 +0.91%, Dow +0.24%, Nasdaq +1.18% — without one clearly dominating. Small caps moved roughly in line with the broader market, with the Russell 2000 at +0.64%. The VIX fell 0.70% to 15.70, which reads as an orderly rotation rather than panic-driven selling. Gold slipped 0.06% to $4,174.50, crude oil was little changed at $68.54, and bitcoin gained 0.04% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
